title: Croatia at the 2026 World Cup · lineup & formation | MyLineups
original_text:
Drawn into Group L, Croatia head into the 2026 World Cup in a 3-4-2-1, coached by Zlatko Dalic.

It is a squad drawn from 24 clubs worldwide, averaging 28.3 years, and worth roughly 244.3 million combined. Plot the XI below, then open it in the free builder to shape your own Croatia lineup for the tournament.

Croatia line up for the FIFA World Cup 2026 in Group L in a 3-4-2-1 , with a back 3 and Livakovic in goal.

In goal: Livakovic, Kotarski, and Pandur.

In defence: Gvardiol, Stanisic, Sutalo, Vuskovic, Caleta-Car, Erlic, and Pongracic.

In midfield: Modric, Pasalic, Sucic, Fruk, Jakic, Kovacic, Kramaric, Moro, Pasalic, Sucic, and Vlasic.

In attack: Baturina, Musa, Perisic, Budimir, and Matanovic.

The 26-man squad pulls players from 24 clubs across world football. The starting XI averages 28.3 years and is worth roughly 244.3 million combined.

Zlatko Dalic (CRO) is the coach guiding this side.

Croatia stays tied to the World Cup hub and opens directly in the builder if you want to reshape the tournament XI — free on the web or in the mobile app.

Open Croatia in the free MyLineups builder, reshape the 3-4-2-1, pick your captain and export a clean image.
